---
# Correlate two result sets with join type = left | inner

`| join type = left (<subquery>) as r r.<key> = <key>` joins a subquery's rows onto the main stream on a key. `type = inner` keeps only matched rows; `type = left` keeps all main rows (matched columns null where absent). Verified: joining a per-dept aggregate back onto each row attaches the group's count/total to every member. IR use: enrich live `xdr_data` events with a lookup of known-bad indicators, or attach a per-host event count to each event. GOTCHA: in a left join, you CANNOT filter unmatched rows with `filter r.<col> = null` — the joined column isn't exposed as `r.<col>` for non-matches and you get `unknown field r.<col>`. For 'rows with no match' (anti-join), aggregate-and-compare or use a presence flag column inside the subquery instead.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_remote_ip != null
| join type = inner (dataset = threat_intel_lookup | fields bad_ip, threat_name) as r r.bad_ip = action_remote_ip
| fields _time, actor_process_image_name, action_remote_ip, threat_name
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
